approvalNote: Peter authorised a real bounded ElevenLabs spend experiment on 2026-06-02.
targetAudience: Tradespeople who remember the job but forget the weird site details until too late.
intent: Test whether ElevenLabs can produce a whole multi-speaker advert with dialogue timing and dog callback beats in one request.
tone: Humble site comedy. JobDone helps you remember the practical details, but life stays messy, funny, and human.

tradesperson.voiceDescription: Happy chappy, warm, upbeat, 'alright governor' energy, extra relaxed because JobDone is helping him remember the job details. Natural and grounded, not a comedy caricature.

app_voice.voiceDescription: Clear calm assistant voice, neutral British delivery, concise and useful, not robotic.

narrator.voiceDescription: Choose the voice most likely to work in a sales advert. Preferred flavour: very calm English actress voice, warm, understated, elegant, a bit sexy in a subtle grown-up way, grounded, no hype, not breathy or cheesy.
narrator.voiceSelectionPriority: Commercial/sales effectiveness first; calm English actress flavour second.

inputs.speaker: tradesperson
inputs.text: [cheerful, calling back through a doorway, relaxed and upbeat] Alright then, Mrs Jones. Bye. [front door shuts]

inputs.speaker: tradesperson
inputs.text: [small friendly device beep: recording starts] [happy chappy, into phone, pleased with himself] Replaced the kitchen sink washer. Isolation valve is stiff here. [small friendly device beep: note saved]

inputs.speaker: tradesperson
inputs.text: [small dog bark behind the closed door] [remembering one more useful detail] Oh, and Charlie the dog bites ankles.

inputs.speaker: narrator
inputs.text: [calm English actress narrator, subtly sensual, dreamy retro TV time-jump sting, playful but original] Doodle doo doo. Doodle doo doo. Doodle doo doo. Three months later...

inputs.speaker: app_voice
inputs.text: [footsteps approach the same front door, then a doorbell rings] Results for Mrs Jones. Kitchen sink washer replaced. Isolation valve stiff. Charlie the dog bites ankles. [small clean device beep: result found]

inputs.speaker: tradesperson
inputs.text: [hushed, relaxed, confident, almost to himself] Hmph, Charlie.

inputs.speaker: narrator
inputs.text: [calm English actress narrator, subtly warm] JobDone. Remember the job.

inputs.speaker: tradesperson
inputs.text: [in the distance, one eager dog bark, footsteps scramble, a small kerfuffle as Charlie steals something] [calling back as the kerfuffle trails off] Oi, Charlie! Come back with that.

truthChecks: Does not claim invoicing, accounting, CRM replacement, or always-correct AI.
The dog detail is captured as ordinary job context, not magic inference.
The ending stays humble: JobDone remembers the job, then life carries on with Charlie.
The one-shot generation is an experiment; editable production assets may still be needed.
